content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

Clean, well-executed tracing instrumentation. All 794 tests pass, cargo clippy -D warnings passes, cargo fmt --check passes. All functions specified in the bead scope have instrumentation. No existing tracing calls removed. No behavioral changes beyond internal return-type adjustments to surface summary counts for the reconcile_success log. Structured fields are used consistently. The only finding is a minor log-level inconsistency for load_controller (INFO instead of DEBUG, unlike peer load functions in the same changeset).

## Amendments

1. load_controller in src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs (line 790) uses the default INFO level for its #[tracing::instrument] span and tracing::info! log (line 803), but peer read/load functions in the same changeset (load_milestone, load_snapshot, read_journal, read_bead_lineage, list_tasks_for_milestone) all use level = "debug". load_controller is a pure state-read with no side effects, making it an 'internal' operation per the acceptance criteria ('DEBUG for internals'). Fix: change the attribute to #[tracing::instrument(skip_all, level = "debug", fields(milestone_id = %milestone_id))] and change the inner log from tracing::info! to tracing::debug!.
   Rationale: Consistency with peer load/read functions in the same changeset. INFO-level logging for a frequent read-only operation adds noise to production logs without diagnostic value, since the operation is purely internal state hydration.
